title: New EU Space Act draft seen as a step backward
summary: A revised draft of the EU Space Act is criticized for being a step backward, creating uncertainty about its application outside the EU. Critics argue that the new version lacks clarity and may hinder international cooperation.

title: D2D services are at risk of becoming too complicated and siloed
summary: Direct-to-Device (D2D) satellite services face complexity and fragmentation risks despite growing partnerships between providers and mobile network operators. Large-scale implementation may be hindered by siloed solutions.

title: Qingzhou prototype cargo spacecraft completes rendezvous tests in orbit
summary: China's Qingzhou prototype cargo spacecraft has successfully completed rendezvous and proximity operations tests in orbit with a satellite, marking a significant step toward developing low-cost orbital infrastructure.

title: India’s TakeMe2Space sets sights on 50-kilowatt data center
summary: Indian startup TakeMe2Space plans to raise $55 million for a 50-kilowatt orbital data center after securing a $5 million seed round. The company aims to demonstrate its capability in the global orbital data center market.

title: Countering missile threats ‘left of launch’
summary: U.S. government agencies are collaborating with industry partners to develop pre-launch missile disruption tools, referred to as 'left of launch' strategies. Raytheon Intelligence and Space is actively involved in addressing evolving missile threats.

title: Taiwan floats shared satcom constellation amid calls for more space collaboration
summary: Taiwan's space agency chief proposed forming a shared communications satellite constellation with other countries to compete with networks like Starlink. The initiative aims to enhance strategic collaboration in space amid growing demand for satellite communications.

title: Companies make the case for commercial space station markets
summary: Companies developing commercial space stations argue that a market for such stations already exists, countering NASA's claim that the market has not yet developed.

title: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space team on new mid-size satellite
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space are developing a new mid-size satellite platform to compete in the emerging 'micro GEO' market for communications and sensing. The platform aims to address growing demand in the space-based services sector.

title: All Points signs agreement to build payload processing facilities at KSC
summary: All Points Logistics has signed an agreement with NASA’s Kennedy Space Center to build satellite processing facilities on center property. This collaboration aims to enhance payload processing capabilities at KSC.

title: How space weather forecasting keeps astronauts (and satellites) safe
summary: The Space Weather Prediction Center (SWPC) in Boulder, Colorado, part of NOAA, works to protect astronauts and satellites from harmful space weather events like solar storms and flares. This effort is crucial for missions such as Artemis 2 and the safety of orbital assets.

title: Artemis 2's heat shield seems to have aced its trial by fire
summary: The heat shield of Artemis 2's Orion capsule successfully endured its re-entry trial through Earth's atmosphere. Mission commander Reid Wiseman confirmed the heat shield performed exceptionally well.

title: Interstellar invader comet 3I/ATLAS made a startling transformation as it passed the sun
summary: The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS underwent significant changes during its passage by the sun in fall 2023, according to astronomers' observations.

title: This giant telescope could discover habitable exoplanets and secrets of our universe — if it gets its funding
summary: The Giant Magellan Telescope in Chile is advancing to its final design phase, with developers seeking funding from Congress to proceed. The telescope aims to discover habitable exoplanets and uncover secrets of the universe.

title: Landing astronauts on the moon 'is absolutely doable, and it's doable soon,' NASA's Artemis 2 commander says
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander stated that landing astronauts on the moon is feasible and imminent. The crew reported enhanced familiarity with the Orion capsule, which could benefit future Artemis missions.

title: 'That was a wonderful toilet': Artemis 2 commander defends mission's lunar loo
summary: Artemis 2 mission commander Reid Wiseman defended the spacecraft's new lunar toilet against criticism, calling it 'a wonderful toilet.' The comments come after the toilet faced negative attention in recent weeks.

title: Fantasy RPG 'Crimson Desert' lets you stab your way up to space, showcasing a remarkably detailed cosmos
summary: The fantasy RPG 'Crimson Desert' features a unique gameplay mechanic allowing players to 'stab their way up to the edge of space'. The game showcases a highly detailed and imaginative cosmos.

title: Blue Origin fires up used New Glenn rocket ahead of landmark reflight (photo)
summary: Blue Origin successfully conducted a static fire test with a previously flown New Glenn rocket booster on April 16, preparing for its first reflight mission scheduled for April 19. This marks a significant milestone in reusable rocket technology.

title: The Lyrid meteor shower 2026 has begun! Here's what to expect
summary: The Lyrid meteor shower of 2026 has begun, with potential sightings of fireballs and meteors when the constellation Lyra is visible. This celestial event offers a natural spectacle for skywatchers.

title: This may be the daughter of one of our universe's 1st stars. Scientists call it an 'ancient immigrant'
summary: Scientists have identified a star escaping the Large Magellanic Cloud into the Milky Way, possibly originating from one of the universe's first stars. This 'ancient immigrant' star provides insights into early stellar populations.

title: Private Japanese spacecraft will inspect 2 dead satellites in 2027
summary: Astroscale's 2027 ISSA-J mission will inspect two dead satellites, marking the first attempt to inspect multiple spacecraft across different orbits in a single flight.

title: NASA Invites Media to Latvia Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: Latvia is set to sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman. The event will include Latvia’s minister for education and science and the chargé d’affaires at the Latvian Embassy to the U.S.

title: At the Edge of Light
summary: A photograph taken on April 6, 2026, captures the Moon's far side near the terminator, highlighting long shadows and the Orientale Basin. The image showcases lunar surface details under low-angle sunlight.

title: Eyeing the Richat Structure
summary: The Richat Structure, a circular geologic feature in northwestern Africa, is easily recognizable from space despite being hard to see from the ground.

title: I Am Artemis: Rebekah Tolatovicz
summary: Rebekah Tolatovicz, a mechanical technician lead, discusses her role in building the Orion spacecraft for NASA's Artemis missions at Kennedy Space Center. She works with Lockheed Martin, the main contractor for Orion, contributing to its assembly, integration, and testing.

title: NASA Selects Voyager for Seventh Private Mission to Space Station
summary: NASA has selected Voyager Technologies for the seventh private astronaut mission to the International Space Station, targeting a launch no earlier than 2028 from Florida. This marks the company's first selection for such a mission, highlighting NASA's commitment to commercial space economy growth.

title: NASA Launches Six CubeSats to International Space Station
summary: NASA launched six CubeSats to the International Space Station (ISS) as part of the Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission. The CubeSats, built by U.S. educational institutions, were among 11,000 pounds of cargo sent aboard the Cygnus XL spacecraft.

title: 2026 NSTA Hyperwall Schedule
summary: NASA announced its participation in the 2026 NSTA Hyperwall Schedule, featuring Hyperwall storytelling by experts from April 16-18, 2026. The event will include sessions on space weather, NASA resources, and the Artemis mission at Booth #1265.

title: NASA’s SPHEREx Mission Maps Water Ice Throughout Cygnus X
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped water ice and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons in Cygnus X, a highly active star-forming region in the Milky Way. The observations highlight the chemical composition of this turbulent area.

title: ‘Interstellar Glaciers’: NASA’s SPHEREx Maps Vast Galactic Ice Regions
summary: NASA's SPHEREx mission has mapped vast interstellar ice regions in the Milky Way galaxy, covering areas over 600 light-years across. The ice was discovered within giant molecular clouds, which are dense regions of gas and dust.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and saw vehicle departures in March. The month was marked by significant activities onboard the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Ship 39 Completes Static fire ahead of Booster 19 test Wednesday
summary: Ship 39 completed a static fire test in preparation for the Booster 19 test scheduled for Wednesday. This follows the successful return of Orion, marking a shift in focus for the Artemis program.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows the recent completion of Northrop Grumman's Cygnus S.S. William McCool CRS NG-23 mission.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining plans for future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing efforts to support future missions.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II set a record by conducting a lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft named Integrity, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission nears its midpoint.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news item without providing specific details in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process through the SME Forum 2026, which includes in-person and online sessions. The event will take place at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026, with preparatory online forums in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement has been highlighted as ideal for navigation purposes.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution videos of the Sun's corona. The mission's first results reveal that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at ESA's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved directional control systems. This innovation could enhance mission precision and efficiency in space operations.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, showed that lasers can propel graphene aerogels with controlled acceleration, unlocking potential for future space applications like solar sail steering.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment simulating space conditions demonstrated the material's potential for future missions.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. The chip's versatility could enhance satellite and spacecraft capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market, aiming to vertically integrate and address scale concerns for customers. The company's CFO, Adam Spice, highlighted the need for integration to ensure reliability in large-scale production.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is preparing to fly its first qualification spacesuit in 2027, with production already underway. The announcement comes despite ongoing discussions or uncertainties about the suit's development.

title: Voyager Technologies Wins Its First Private ISS Mission
summary: Voyager Technologies has secured its first private mission to the International Space Station (ISS). Axiom Space previously dominated NASA's PAM awards but faces new competition in 2026.

title: Seraphim Space Aims To Shape Strategy With New Advisory Council
summary: Seraphim Space has formed a new advisory council to address key challenges in the space industry, including sustainability, regulation, national security, and international collaboration.

title: ORBX ETF Launches Amid Wave of Space Tech IPOs
summary: ORBX ETF launches to track companies earning over half their revenue from space tech, including transportation, software, and satellite services. This follows a trend of increased space tech IPOs.

title: Kepler Leads Team to Test ESA’s ‘Fiber in the Sky’
summary: ESA has selected a team led by Kepler Communications to test the 'fiber in the sky' HydRON orbital communications network. The initiative aims to demonstrate advanced optical communications in space.

title: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman On Artemis, Budget, And Establishing a Lasting Space Vision
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman discusses the Artemis program, budget challenges, and the need for a sustainable space vision. He highlights the gap between scientific passion and financial execution in space missions.

title: CLD Companies Say NASA Is Wrong. NASA Says Prove It.
summary: Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) providers dispute NASA's claims, while NASA challenges them to prove their objections wrong. The disagreement was highlighted during discussions at the Space Symposium.

title: Slingshot Introduces AI-Powered Portal Platform
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched an AI-powered platform enabling operators to detect anomalies and make decisions through a centralized portal. The platform aims to streamline space operations with intelligent automation.

title: ICON Prime Launches, Led By Former TX Rep
summary: ICON Prime has been launched as a new business unit by ICON, a robotic construction company, with collaborations planned with the Pentagon and NASA. The initiative is led by a former Texas representative.

title: Space Force, NATO Commanders Share Threat Perspectives
summary: U.S. Space Force is actively identifying new space capabilities and monitoring threats from adversaries while creating missions to defend assets. NATO commanders shared their threat perspectives in a discussion.

title: Air Force Awarded Development Contract for Space-Based AMTI; Meink Says Risk Low
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force awarded a contract to multiple vendors for the development of space-based air moving target indication (AMTI). The move is seen as having low risk according to Meink.

title: Boeing and Millennium Space Systems Add Mid-Class Resolute Satellite Bus
summary: Boeing and its subsidiary Millennium Space Systems have introduced a new mid-class satellite platform named Resolute, offering enhanced capabilities compared to traditional small satellites.

title: Slingshot Aerospace Releases AI-Powered Analytics Platform for Space Operations
summary: Slingshot Aerospace launched Portal, an AI-powered analytics platform for satellite data monitoring and analysis. The software integrates Slingshot’s data flows with external sources to enhance space operations.

title: Industry Builds the Case Around Architecture Options for Orbital Data Centers
summary: The space industry is exploring orbital data centers as a viable alternative to terrestrial facilities due to lower satellite launch costs and growing demand. This shift aims to provide new infrastructure solutions amid rising needs for data processing and storage.

title: Transport Layer of PWSA Will Eventually Transition From SDA to New Acquisition Executive, Sandhoo Says
summary: The Space Force plans to transfer the communications layer of the Space Development Agency's Proliferated Warfighter Space Architecture (PWSA) to a new acquisition executive. This transition is part of the Space Force's broader acquisition structure changes.

title: COMSO Targets Expanding Working Capital Fund and Determining CASR Funding
summary: Space Systems Command’s Commercial Space Office (COMSO) is exploring ways to expand its Working Capital Fund and determine funding for the Commercial Assessment of Space Resiliency (CASR). The initiative aims to leverage commercial capabilities within the space industry.

title: Teledyne Space Imaging President Megan Tremer Shares Enabling Tech for Artemis II
summary: Megan Tremer, President of Space Imaging at Teledyne, discusses the technologies provided by Teledyne for the Artemis II mission during an interview at Space Symposium.

title: Moog Space SVP Mark Covelli Details Meteor Satellite Bus at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Space's senior vice president Mark Covelli discussed the company's support for the Artemis II mission and its investments in the Meteor satellite bus during the Space Symposium.

title: Lonestar Expands Launch Plans for Orbital Data Storage Payload
summary: Lonestar announced the launch date for StarVault, the world's first commercially operational space-based sovereign data storage platform. The company highlighted growing demand for off-planet data storage solutions.
